Title: Darkbloom — Private AI Inference on Apple Silicon
Description: Decentralized inference on hardware-verified Apple Silicon. End-to-end encrypted. The node operator never sees your data. OpenAI-compatible — change one line.

Title: Friends Don't Let Friends Use Ollama | Sleeping Robots
Description: Ollama gained traction by being the first easy llama.cpp wrapper, then spent years dodging attribution, misleading users, and pivoting to cloud, all while riding VC money earned on someone else's engine. Here's the full history, and why the alternatives are better.

Title: Burgers | マクドナルド公式
Description: Check out McDonald's new, limited and regular burger menus for breakfast, regular and dinner time.  Order and pay in advance on your smartphone without waiting at the cashier and just pick up at store nearby.

Title: Rome Is Burning
Description: Three fires in one week. A Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's mansion, an arson at a toilet paper factory, a mall ablaze. The common thread is not crime. It's what happens when you replace human judgment with spreadsheets.

Title: Startups are context arbitrages
Description: If all code will be written by LLMs, build vs buy will simply be a context arbitrage between startups and their customers.

Title: Designing Binary Encodings for JSON and VARIANT
Description: In this post, we’ll dig into the internal designs of binary encoding for JSON. There’s been little discussion of the trade-offs these encodings make. We’ll see how even a straightforward binary encoding can materially improve retrieval performance.

Title: I Suddenly Lost My Enthusiasm for Interneting
Description: Why the internet feels like total garbage. Why the literacy level of the content available online looks, to me, like it decreases exponentially.

Title: I Did My Taxes with Claude. Here's What Happened.
Description:
It’s tax day — so, shoutout to my tax friends. I hope you get some sleep tonight.

I got mine done. Twice, actually: once with Claude, once with TurboTax, then reconciled the two side-by-side to the dollar. I was curious how AI would handle a workflow we all know — and

Description: It is April in Cyprus and the windows are open. I have been sitting with a coffee that went cold twenty minutes ago, thinking about a conversation I had in January last year, on a video call from the desk in my home office in Düsseldorf, with a manager who

Title: Writing an LLM from scratch, part 32k -- Interventions: training a better model locally with gradient accumulation
Description: Having worked out which combination of interventions into my model and training run improved loss the most, it was time to see how well that worked with a local run, which meant I had to learn about gradient accumulation

Title: Introduction
Description: Gym Anything is a framework for building and running computer-use environments.

Description: I’ve been messing around with local LLMs on my 3090 for a while now — I have a growing collection of Qwen models on D:LLM that I probably should be embarrassed about. A few weeks ago I stumbled across David Noel Ng’s LLM Neuroanatomy blog posts, where he showed that you can take a pretrained […]

Title: Daniel's Blog · Rules, Not Weights
Description: Mainstream ML fixes the scoring rule and trains the weights. We're exploring the opposite — searching the rule.

Title: We Are in This Together
Description: Harper's mission is to encourage and assist people with communicating their complex ideas and emotions.

Title: Fun with EML (Exp-Minus-Log)
Description: A paper is proposing that most elementary functions such as sin, cos, 1/x can be expressed as a combination of the following function and the constant 1. eml(x,y)=exp(x)−ln(y)eml(x,y) = exp(x) &#82…

Title: My 14-Year Journey Away from ORMs - How I Built pGenie, the SQL-First Postgres Code Generator
Description: Hi, I’m Nikita Volkov - architect, consultant, and the author of hasql, one of the two main PostgreSQL drivers in Haskell used in major production projects like PostgREST and IHP. After 25 years in IT and more late-night schema-drift fires than I care to count, I open-sourced something I wish had existed a decade ago: pGenie.

Title: The Great Software Meltdown
Description: Naval called it. Software was eaten by AI.— Naval (@naval) March 14, 2026 And the market already agreed. Atlassian down 75%. HubSpot down 69%. Figma

Title: I don't care that it's X times faster
Description: Many new project announcements these days come with a tagline of 'X times faster than ...' and I need to rant about it.

Title: Things you didn't know about indexes
Description: Let’s start with things you probably did know.

Title: 🚂 Temporal — Under the Hood
Description: What actually happens when you start a Temporal workflow? We trace every SQL statement, count the event-history nodes, and then watch Absurd — a 5-table, single-SQL-file system — do the same job with one-fifth the queries and twenty-times the throughput.

Title: I Connected a Desktop Phone to a FreeBSD Server, so Now I Can Call It
Description: I turned a Panasonic KX-T2315 desk phone into a physical menu for my FreeBSD server. When I pick up the handset, the phone adapter calls Asterisk, waits for one digit, and triggers a predefined script on the FreeBSD server. This post covers the phone restoration, HT801 setup, Asterisk configuration, Lua dialplan, Python AGI dispatcher, and the full config download.

Title: How Wispr Flow Ate My Spacebar: A Forensic Investigation Into Silent Keyboard Interception
Description: A deep forensic investigation into how Wispr Flow silently intercepts every keystroke via CGEventTap, suppressed 145 spacebar presses, tracks every app and URL, and uploads data even with sharing disabled.

Title: Turning Meshes into Horrifying Piecewise Functions
Description: I recently wrote a program, mesh2frep, that is, honestly, a bit unhinged at best. Let me tell you about it!

Title: Make It Myself
Finding a duplicated item in an array of N integers in the range 1 to N − 1 - The Old New Thing
Description: Taking advantage of special characteristics of the array.

Title: Open Source Isn't Dead. | Strix
Description: AI has changed vulnerability discovery, but closing source code does not remove the attack surface. Continuous AI defense is the better response.

Title: Google Broke Its Promise to Me. Now ICE Has My Data.
Description: In 2025, Google gave Amandla Thomas-Johnson's data to ICE without giving him the chance to challenge the subpoena, breaking a nearly decade-long promise to notify users before handing their data to law enforcement.

Title: Your Backpack Got Worse On Purpose
Description: In 1986, a corporation that made women's lingerie bought every backpack brand you've ever trusted.

Title: God sleeps in the minerals
Description: I took this snapshot, and the rest of them, at the Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County’s Unearthed: Raw Beauty exhibition yesterday. Enjoy.

Title: Want to Write a Compiler? Just Read These Two Papers.
Description: Imagine you don't know anything about programming, and you want learn how to do it.  You take a look at Amazon.com, and there's a highly recommended set of books by Knute or something with a promising title, The Art of Computer Programming, so you buy them.  Now imagine that it's more than just a poor choice, but that all the books on programming are at written at that level.

Title: An Arm Mainboard for the Framework Laptop
Description:
Using the repair-friendly Framework 13 laptop chassis, I've tested the low-end x86 option (a Ryzen AI 5 340 Mainboard), the fastest RISC-V option (DC-ROMA II), and today I'm publishing results from the only Arm Mainboard, the MetaComputing AI PC, which has a 12-core Arm SoC and up to 32 GB of soldered-on RAM.
My Framework 13 has run on x86, RISC-V, and now Arm, making it something of a 'Ship of Theseus'.
